title: Crumbly Tangy Plantain Crumble
description: This tangy and crumbly dish features capers as a key ingredient, designed to reduce the effects of a hangover in Venezuelan cuisine.

ingredients.name: capers (preserved in salt or water-based brine)

instructions: Peel, slice the plantains into thin layers and sprinkle with a pinch of salt.
Melt butter in a pan over medium heat. Add sliced plantain layers and cook until golden brown on both sides.
Mix lemon juice, vinegar, fresh dill, and capers with finely ground sugar or coarse sugar if desired. Adjust seasoning based on taste preferences.
Pour the mixture evenly over cooked plantains in the pan and mix gently to ensure even coverage.
Transfer the dish into a preheated oven for 10-15 minutes until the top is crispy and slightly caramelized.
Garnish with fresh dill before serving. Serve warm as a dessert accompaniment.
